title: Dutch Rye Flatbread
description: A crispy flatbread infused with the earthy flavors of Dutch rye, perfect for snacking or as a side.

ingredients.name: Dutch rye flour
ingredients.notes: use a high-quality Dutch rye flour for the best flavor

ingredients.name: water
ingredients.notes: use lukewarm water for better yeast activation

ingredients.name: icy cold butter
ingredients.notes: use a high-quality, icy cold butter for a flaky crust

ingredients.name: salt
ingredients.notes: use a flaky sea salt for added texture and flavor

ingredients.name: energizing yeast
ingredients.notes: use a high-quality, energizing yeast for better rising

instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine the Dutch rye flour, salt, and energizing yeast.
Gradually add the lukewarm water, mixing until a shaggy dough forms.
Add the icy cold butter, mixing until the dough comes together in a cohesive mass.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes, until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a lightly oiled bowl, cover it with plastic wrap, and let it rise in a warm place for 1 hour.
Punch down the dough and divide it into 4-6 equal pieces, depending on desired flatbread size.
Roll out each piece into a thin circle, about 1/8 inch thick.
Place the flatbreads on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper, leaving about 1 inch of space between each flatbread.
Bake the flatbreads for 15-20 minutes, until they are golden brown and crispy.
Remove the flatbreads from the oven and let them cool on a wire rack for 5 minutes before serving.
